cards.title: Thyroid Disorders — Naturopathic Approach
cards._legacyHtml:
ParameterHypothyroidHyperthyroidSubclinical Hypo
TSH↑ (>4.5 mIU/L)↓ (<0.4 mIU/L)4.5–10 mIU/L
Free T4↓↑Normal
Free T3↓↑Normal
SymptomsFatigue, weight gain, cold, constipation, dry skin, bradycardia, depressionWeight loss, heat, tremor, palpitations, diarrhoea, anxiety, exophthalmosFatigue, weight gain (mild)
Common causeHashimoto's (TPO Ab+), iodine deficiencyGraves' (TSH-R Ab+), toxic noduleEarly Hashimoto's, iodine deficiency
N
Naturopathic Support — Hypothyroid Selenium 200mcg/day (cofactor for T4→T3 conversion, reduces TPO Ab in Hashimoto's). Iodine (from food — seaweed, fish — not high-dose supplements in autoimmune). Zinc 15mg (thyroid hormone synthesis). Ashwagandha (improves T4, T3 levels — RCT evidence). Avoid: raw goitrogens (broccoli, kale — cooking deactivates). Gluten-free diet in Hashimoto's with coeliac overlap.
H
Naturopathic Support — Hyperthyroid Lemon balm (Melissa officinalis) — inhibits TSH binding. Bugleweed (Lycopus) — reduces T4 production. Motherwort — for palpitations. L-carnitine 2g/day — antagonises thyroid hormone at tissue level. Note: these are ADJUNCTS to medical treatment — never replace antithyroid drugs or RAI in Graves' without physician agreement.
🟡 Hashimoto's Pearl
Selenium 200mcg/day reduces TPO antibody titres by 40–60% in Hashimoto's — Grade A evidence. This is the most evidence-based naturopathic intervention for autoimmune thyroid disease. Combine with gluten-free diet (if HLA-DQ2/DQ8 positive or coeliac screen positive) and vitamin D3 4000 IU/day. Magnesium 300mg helps with fatigue and constipation. Monitor TSH every 6–12 months.

cards.title: Adrenal & Cortisol — Functional Assessment
cards._legacyHtml:
HPA Axis Dysregulation
"Adrenal fatigue" is not an accepted medical diagnosis but HPA axis dysregulation is real and measurable. Distinguish from Addison's disease (autoimmune adrenal failure) which requires urgent medical management.
PatternCortisol ProfileSymptomsNaturopathic Protocol
Stage 1 — High cortisol (alarm)High morning, stays elevatedAnxiety, insomnia, central weight gain, hypertensionAdaptogens (Ashwagandha, Rhodiola), Mg, L-theanine, meditation, reduce stimulants
Stage 2 — Mixed (resistance)Flat pattern, normal range but no diurnal rhythmFatigue + anxiety, afternoon crash, poor sleepAdrenal support: Vit C 1g BD, B5 500mg, Mg, Holy Basil, regular meal timing
Stage 3 — Low cortisol (exhaustion)Low morning, very flat curveExtreme fatigue, low BP, salt craving, poor stress responseSame as Stage 2 + licorice root (raises cortisol 40%) + protein breakfast + REST
🟡 Testing
Gold standard: 4-point salivary cortisol (7am, 12pm, 5pm, 10pm) — assesses diurnal rhythm. Better than single serum morning cortisol (misses pattern dysregulation). DHEA-S (adrenal reserve marker) is often low in stage 3. Always exclude Addison's (primary adrenal failure) first with ACTH stimulation test before attributing to HPA dysregulation. Red flags: weight loss, hyperpigmentation, severe postural hypotension → Addison's until proven otherwise.

cards.title: PCOS — Comprehensive Naturopathic Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Rotterdam Criteria — 2 of 3
(1) Oligo/anovulation (irregular periods) + (2) Clinical/biochemical hyperandrogenism (hirsutism, acne, elevated testosterone) + (3) Polycystic ovaries on ultrasound (≥12 follicles per ovary or ovary >10mL).
InterventionTargetDoseEvidence
Inositol (Myo + D-chiro 40:1)Insulin resistance, ovulation4g myo-inositol + 100mg D-chiro/day★★★★ Strong — restores ovulation in 70%
BerberineInsulin resistance, androgen500mg TDS with meals★★★ Good — equals metformin in some trials
Spearmint teaAnti-androgenic2 cups/day (1 tsp dried)★★★ Good — reduces free testosterone
Vitex agnus-castusLH:FSH ratio, progesterone400–500mg standardised extract OD★★★ Good — regulates cycle
N-Acetyl CysteineInsulin resistance, ovulation600mg TDS★★★ Good — comparable to metformin
ZincAnti-androgenic, insulin30–50mg/day★★★ Good — reduces hirsutism, improves IR
Vit D3Insulin resistance, menstrual regularity4000–6000 IU/day★★★ Good — PCOS patients often severely deficient
Low-GI diet + exerciseInsulin resistance, weight150 min/week + low-GI, high-fibre★★★★ Strong — 5% weight loss restores ovulation in 50%

cards.title: Type 2 Diabetes — Naturopathic Reversal Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
T2DM is Reversible
DiRECT trial: 46% remission at 1 year, 36% at 2 years with low-calorie diet. Naturopathic approach targets all modifiable drivers simultaneously.
InterventionHbA1c ReductionEvidenceProtocol
Low-carb diet (<130g/day)1.0–1.5%StrongReduce refined carbs first. Mediterranean or LCHF pattern.
Weight loss (10%)1.0%StrongEvery kg lost approximately 0.1% HbA1c reduction
Aerobic + resistance exercise0.6–0.7%Strong150 min aerobic + 2x resistance/week. Post-meal walks 15 min reduce glucose spike 30%.
Berberine0.9–1.0%Strong — comparable to metformin500mg TDS with meals. Activates AMPK.
Cinnamon (Ceylon only)0.3–0.5%Good1–3g/day. Ceylon only — Cassia has hepatotoxic coumarin.
Alpha lipoic acid0.3–0.5%Good600mg/day. Also treats neuropathy.
Magnesium0.3%Good400mg Mg glycinate/day. 50% of T2DM are Mg-deficient.
Intermittent fasting (16:8)0.5–1.0%GoodNot if on insulin (hypoglycaemia risk — physician supervision required)
🟡 Post-Meal Walking
15 minutes of walking within 30 minutes of eating reduces postprandial glucose by 30% — equivalent to metformin for postprandial control. Mechanism: contracting muscles take up glucose independently of insulin (GLUT4 translocation). Prescribe: walk 15 min after each meal. Free, simple, powerful. Monitor HbA1c 3-monthly and reduce medications with physician if targets achieved through lifestyle.

cards.title: Menopause — Naturopathic Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
SymptomInterventionDoseEvidence
Hot flushes / night sweatsBlack cohosh (Actaea racemosa)40–80mg standardised extract/dayGood — reduces frequency 50%
Hot flushesSoy isoflavones / Red clover40–80mg isoflavones/dayGood — reduces severity
Mood / anxietySt John's Wort + Black cohoshStandard combined doseGood RCT for combination
Vaginal drynessSea buckthorn oil oral + topical Vit E3g/day oral + topicalGood
SleepValerian + Lemon balm + Mg glycinate300mg each at bedtimeGood
Bone protectionCa 1200mg + D3 2000IU + K2 100mcg + Mg 400mgAs stated dailyStrong
CVD protectionMediterranean diet + exercise + omega-3 3gDailyStrong
🟡 Black Cohosh Safety
Black cohosh is NOT oestrogenic — it works via serotonin and dopamine pathways. Safe in oestrogen-receptor-positive breast cancer (unlike phytoestrogens — conflicting data for ER+ cancer). Rare hepatotoxicity (1:1,000,000) — monitor LFTs with long-term use. Use maximum 6 months then reassess. Do not confuse with blue cohosh (Caulophyllum) — different plant, potentially toxic. Avoid in pregnancy and liver disease.

cards.title: Insulin Resistance & Metabolic Syndrome
cards._legacyHtml:
Insulin Resistance — The Root of Modern Chronic Disease
IR underlies T2DM, PCOS, NAFLD, dyslipidaemia, hypertension, and CVD together forming Metabolic Syndrome. Identifying and reversing IR early is the single most impactful preventive intervention.
Metabolic Syndrome Criteria (IDF)ThresholdTarget
Central obesity (waist)≥90cm (M) / ≥80cm (F) Asian<80cm (M) / <75cm (F)
Fasting triglycerides≥1.7 mmol/L<1.0 mmol/L
HDL-C<1.0 (M) / <1.3 (F) mmol/L>1.4 (M) / >1.7 (F)
Blood pressure≥130/85<120/80
Fasting glucose≥5.6 mmol/L<5.0 mmol/L
1
HOMA-IR DiagnosisHOMA-IR = (Fasting insulin µIU/mL × Fasting glucose mmol/L) ÷ 22.5. <1.0 = optimal. 1–1.9 = early IR. 2–2.9 = moderate. ≥3 = significant. ≥4 = severe. HOMA-IR >2.0 present in 60% of people with normal fasting glucose — IR is invisible on standard tests. Fasting insulin alone: >15 µIU/mL = IR.
2
Diet ProtocolLow-GL diet ★★★. Remove: refined carbs, sugar, juice, ultra-processed, trans fats, alcohol. Add: fibre 25–35g/day, ACV 1 tbsp before meals (↓ postprandial glucose 20–35% ★★★), cinnamon 1–3g/day ★★. TRE 16:8 — reduces insulin secretion, improves receptor sensitivity.
3
Supplement ProtocolBerberine 500mg TID ★★★ (AMPK activator — comparable to metformin). ALA 600mg/day ★★★. Magnesium 400mg ★★★ (deficient in 90% of MetS). Chromium picolinate 200–400mcg ★★. Myo-inositol 2g + D-chiro 50mg ★★★ (PCOS-IR). CoQ10 200mg. Omega-3 3g/day.
🟡 IR-First Approach
Before treating PCOS, NAFLD, prediabetes, hypertension, or dyslipidaemia — screen for IR first. IR is upstream of all these. Correcting IR often resolves downstream problems. HOMA-IR should be in every first-appointment blood panel for any patient with central obesity, fatigue, sugar cravings, skin tags, or acanthosis nigricans.

cards.title: Hypothyroidism vs Hashimoto's — Deep Dive
cards._legacyHtml:
Critical Distinction
Hypothyroidism is a functional state. Hashimoto's is an autoimmune disease that causes it. 90% of hypothyroidism in iodine-sufficient regions is Hashimoto's. Every hypothyroid patient needs TPO antibody testing — they require immune modulation, not just thyroid hormone replacement.
ParameterHypothyroidismHashimoto's
AntibodiesVariableAnti-TPO 95%+, Anti-TG 80%
UltrasoundVariableHeterogeneous hypoechoic "moth-eaten"
Treatment priorityLevothyroxineAutoimmune control FIRST
Subclinical (TSH 4–10)Treat if symptomatic, TSH>10Selenium reduces Ab even before TSH rises
1
Full Functional PanelTSH + fT4 + fT3 + Reverse T3 + Anti-TPO + Anti-TG. Normal TSH + low fT3 + high rT3 = peripheral conversion failure (stress, inflammation, nutrient deficiency). rT3:fT3 ratio >0.2 = functional hypothyroid despite normal TSH.
2
Hashimoto's ProtocolSelenium 200mcg/day ★★★ (reduces TPO antibodies 50% — Cochrane). Gluten-free trial 12 weeks ★★ (molecular mimicry). Vit D 4000–6000 IU ★★★ (target 100–150 nmol/L). Myo-inositol 600mg BD + selenium: superior to selenium alone (RCT 2019). LDN 1.5–4.5mg bedtime ★★ (TLR4/TLR9 downregulation). Avoid excess iodine in Hashimoto's.
🟡 T3 vs T4 — When to Add T3
20% of hypothyroid patients remain symptomatic on T4 alone (DIO2 gene polymorphism — poor converters). Signs: persistent fatigue, depression, brain fog despite normal TSH. Natural T3 conversion support: selenium 200mcg, zinc 15mg, iron (ferritin >70), stress management. Consider DUTCH test for full thyroid metabolite picture.

cards.title: Hyperthyroidism & Graves' Disease
cards._legacyHtml:
⚠️ Do NOT Manage Without Conventional Oversight
Hyperthyroidism can cause AF, heart failure, thyroid storm, and osteoporosis. Graves' = 70–80% of cases. Naturopathic interventions are adjuncts only — never primary treatment for moderate-severe hyperthyroidism.
FeatureGraves'TMNGToxic Adenoma
Eye disease30% ophthalmopathyAbsentAbsent
TSH receptor AbTRAb positive ★NegativeNegative
Thyroid scanDiffuse ↑ uptakePatchy ↑Hot nodule
1
RecognitionPalpitations, weight loss, heat intolerance, tremor, anxiety, diarrhoea, tachycardia. Graves' specific: exophthalmos, lid retraction, pretibial myxoedema. Thyroid storm: hyperthermia >40°C + extreme tachycardia + confusion = EMERGENCY.
2
Naturopathic AdjunctBugleweed 2ml TID ★★ (TSH receptor inhibition). Lemon balm 300mg TID ★★ (mild antithyroid). L-Carnitine 1–2g/day (peripheral thyroid hormone antagonist). Ca + Vit D (bone protection). Selenium 200mcg ★★★ for mild Graves' ophthalmopathy (EUGOGO trial). Smoking cessation critical — doubles ophthalmopathy severity.

cards.title: Adrenal Fatigue vs HPA Axis Dysfunction
cards._legacyHtml:
Terminology
"Adrenal fatigue" is not an accepted medical diagnosis — but HPA axis dysregulation is real and measurable. Accurate term: HPA axis dysfunction or allostatic overload. Single AM cortisol is inadequate — use 4-point salivary cortisol.
HPA StageCortisol PatternPresentationPriority
Stage 1 — AlarmCortisol ↑↑ all dayWired but tired, insomnia, anxietyAdaptogens (gentle), Mg, phosphatidylserine
Stage 2 — ResistanceHigh AM, low PMMorning OK, 2–4pm crash, light sleepAdaptogens + sleep protocol
Stage 3 — ExhaustionFlat/low all dayProfound fatigue, orthostatic hypotension, salt cravingAdrenal glandulars, DHEA, Vit C 2g, B5
1
Testing4-point salivary cortisol (8am, noon, 4pm, bedtime). DHEA-S. DUTCH test (best — adds metabolites + sex hormones). Optimal AM salivary cortisol: 13–24 nmol/L. Flat CAR = Stage 3/burnout.
2
Stage-Specific AdaptogensStage 1: Ashwagandha KSM-66 300mg BD ★★★ (cortisol ↓ 28%) + Phosphatidylserine 300mg PM + Rhodiola 200mg AM. Stage 2: Ashwagandha + Rhodiola AM + Holy Basil 500mg BD. Stage 3: Licorice root 500mg + Adrenal glandular + Vit C 2g + B5 500mg BD. NEVER stimulants in Stage 3.

cards.title: Diabetes Complications — Naturopathic Prevention
cards._legacyHtml:
ComplicationKey Naturopathic ProtocolEvidence
Diabetic Peripheral NeuropathyALA 600mg oral/IV ★★★, B12 methylcobalamin 1000mcg ★★★ (especially metformin users), Benfotiamine 300mg BD, Acetyl-L-carnitine 2g★★★ ALA Cochrane
Diabetic NephropathyReduce dietary AGEs (poach/steam vs grill). Protein 0.6–0.8g/kg (CKD 3+). Silymarin (milk thistle). NAC. Vit C 500mg + E 400IU. Monitor ACR 6-monthly.★★ Antioxidant renal protection
Diabetic RetinopathyBilberry 160mg BD ★★, Lutein + zeaxanthin 10mg, Pycnogenol 150mg ★★. AVOID: Kapalbhati, inversions (↑ IOP). Annual fundoscopy.★★ Pycnogenol RCT
Diabetic FootDaily 10-point foot inspection. No barefoot walking. Manuka honey dressings ★★★. Treat neuropathy. Tight glucose control.★★★ Prevention protocol
CV DiseaseOmega-3 3–4g ★★★, Mediterranean diet ★★★, BP <130/80, LDL <1.8, CoQ10, exercise 150 min/week ★★★★★★ Multiple landmark trials
🟡 AGE Reduction
Advanced glycation end-products from high-temperature cooking (grilling, frying) add to endogenous AGE formation from hyperglycaemia. Low-AGE methods: poaching, steaming, stewing. Benfotiamine (fat-soluble B1) blocks 3 of 4 major AGE pathways — most underused diabetic complication prevention intervention.

cards.title: Thyroid in Pregnancy
cards._legacyHtml:
⚠️ High Stakes
Untreated hypothyroidism in pregnancy causes miscarriage, preterm birth, and impaired fetal neurodevelopment. Fetal brain depends entirely on maternal thyroid hormones until 12 weeks. Screen ALL pregnant women with TSH at booking.
TSH LevelT1 TargetT2 TargetT3 Target
Upper limit of normal2.5 mIU/L3.0 mIU/L3.5 mIU/L
Treatment thresholdTSH >2.5 + TPO+ = treat. TSH >4.0 = treat regardlessTSH >3.0TSH >3.5
Target on treatment0.1–2.5 mIU/L0.2–3.00.3–3.0
1
ManagementKnown hypothyroid: increase levothyroxine 20–30% immediately on confirmed pregnancy. Monitor TSH every 4 weeks T1, every 4–6 weeks T2–T3. Levothyroxine: empty stomach, separated from prenatal vitamins (iron, calcium) by 4 hours. Naturopathic: selenium 100mcg (safe, reduces postpartum thyroiditis risk). Iodine 150–220mcg/day from prenatal vitamins.
2
Postpartum ThyroiditisAffects 5–10% of women in first year postpartum. Pattern: Hyperthyroid phase (1–4 months) → Hypothyroid phase (4–8 months) → Recovery (6–12 months, 25% permanent). Selenium 200mcg/day reduces PPT risk in TPO+ women ★★★ (Cochrane). Monitor TSH at 3 and 6 months postpartum in TPO+ women.

cards.title: Obesity — Hormonal Drivers & Naturopathic Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Hormonal DriverClinical CluesNaturopathic Approach
Insulin resistanceCentral obesity, acanthosis nigricans, HOMA-IR >2.0Low-GI diet, berberine, ALA, resistance training, TRE
Leptin resistanceNever feel full, high serum leptin, night eatingSleep 8hrs ★★★, omega-3, reduce fructose, zinc
HypothyroidismCold intolerance, constipation, TSH ↑Treat hypothyroidism. Selenium, zinc.
PCOS/HyperandrogenismIrregular periods, hirsutism, elevated DHEASInositol, berberine, low-GI, anti-androgens, exercise
Chronic stress/cortisolVisceral obesity, easy bruising — rule out Cushing'sAshwagandha, phosphatidylserine, stress management
Gut microbiomeDiet doesn't explain weight — dysbiosis patternHigh-fibre diet, diverse plants, probiotics
1
BNYS ProtocolAssessment: identify hormonal driver(s). BMI + waist + HOMA-IR + TSH + cortisol. Diet: Mediterranean, 500 kcal deficit (not >1000). Protein 1.4–1.6g/kg. 30g fibre minimum. Exercise: resistance 3×/week + 10,000 steps + post-meal walks. Sleep 7–9 hours (non-negotiable — sleep deprivation = +300 kcal/day food intake).
🟡 Set Point Theory
After weight loss, compensatory adaptations persist for years: ↑ ghrelin, ↓ leptin, ↓ resting metabolic rate by 15–20%. This explains 95% of caloric restriction diet failure — not a willpower problem. Solutions: slow weight loss (0.5kg/week), long-term programme (12+ months), resistance training (maintains metabolic rate), address psychological drivers.

Diabetic RetinopathyBilberry 160mg BD ★★, Lutein + zeaxanthin 10mg, Pycnogenol 150mg ★★. AVOID: Kapalbhati, inversions (↑ IOP). Annual fundoscopy.★★ Pycnogenol RCT
Diabetic FootDaily 10-point foot inspection. No barefoot walking. Manuka honey dressings ★★★. Treat neuropathy. Tight glucose control.★★★ Prevention protocol
CV DiseaseOmega-3 3–4g ★★★, Mediterranean diet ★★★, BP <130/80, LDL <1.8, CoQ10, exercise 150 min/week ★★★★★★ Multiple landmark trials
🟡 AGE Reduction
Advanced glycation end-products from high-temperature cooking (grilling, frying) add to endogenous AGE formation from hyperglycaemia. Low-AGE methods: poaching, steaming, stewing. Benfotiamine (fat-soluble B1) blocks 3 of 4 major AGE pathways — most underused diabetic complication prevention intervention.

cards.title: Obesity — Hormonal Drivers & Naturopathic Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Hormonal DriverClinical CluesNaturopathic Approach
Insulin resistanceCentral obesity, acanthosis nigricans, HOMA-IR >2.0Low-GI diet, berberine, ALA, resistance training, TRE
Leptin resistanceNever feel full, high serum leptin, night eatingSleep 8hrs ★★★, omega-3, reduce fructose, zinc
HypothyroidismCold intolerance, constipation, TSH ↑Treat hypothyroidism. Selenium, zinc.
PCOS/HyperandrogenismIrregular periods, hirsutism, elevated DHEASInositol, berberine, low-GI, anti-androgens, exercise
Chronic stress/cortisolVisceral obesity, easy bruising — rule out Cushing'sAshwagandha, phosphatidylserine, stress management
Gut microbiomeDiet doesn't explain weight — dysbiosis patternHigh-fibre diet, diverse plants, probiotics
1
BNYS ProtocolAssessment: identify hormonal driver(s). BMI + waist + HOMA-IR + TSH + cortisol. Diet: Mediterranean, 500 kcal deficit (not >1000). Protein 1.4–1.6g/kg. 30g fibre minimum. Exercise: resistance 3×/week + 10,000 steps + post-meal walks. Sleep 7–9 hours (non-negotiable — sleep deprivation = +300 kcal/day food intake).
🟡 Set Point Theory
After weight loss, compensatory adaptations persist for years: ↑ ghrelin, ↓ leptin, ↓ resting metabolic rate by 15–20%. This explains 95% of caloric restriction diet failure — not a willpower problem. Solutions: slow weight loss (0.5kg/week), long-term programme (12+ months), resistance training (maintains metabolic rate), address psychological drivers.

cards.title: Adrenal & Cortisol — Functional Assessment
cards._legacyHtml:
HPA Axis Dysregulation
"Adrenal fatigue" is not an accepted medical diagnosis but HPA axis dysregulation is real and measurable. Distinguish from Addison's disease (autoimmune adrenal failure) which requires urgent medical management.
PatternCortisol ProfileSymptomsNaturopathic Protocol
Stage 1 — High cortisol (alarm)High morning, stays elevatedAnxiety, insomnia, central weight gain, hypertensionAdaptogens (Ashwagandha, Rhodiola), Mg, L-theanine, meditation, reduce stimulants
Stage 2 — Mixed (resistance)Flat pattern, normal range but no diurnal rhythmFatigue + anxiety, afternoon crash, poor sleepAdrenal support: Vit C 1g BD, B5 500mg, Mg, Holy Basil, regular meal timing
Stage 3 — Low cortisol (exhaustion)Low morning, very flat curveExtreme fatigue, low BP, salt craving, poor stress responseSame as Stage 2 + licorice root (raises cortisol 40%) + protein breakfast + REST
🟡 Testing
Gold standard: 4-point salivary cortisol (7am, 12pm, 5pm, 10pm) — assesses diurnal rhythm. Better than single serum morning cortisol (misses pattern dysregulation). DHEA-S (adrenal reserve marker) is often low in stage 3. Always exclude Addison's (primary adrenal failure) first with ACTH stimulation test before attributing to HPA dysregulation. Red flags: weight loss, hyperpigmentation, severe postural hypotension → Addison's until proven otherwise.

cards.title: Adrenal Fatigue vs HPA Axis Dysfunction
cards._legacyHtml:
Terminology
"Adrenal fatigue" is not an accepted medical diagnosis — but HPA axis dysregulation is real and measurable. Accurate term: HPA axis dysfunction or allostatic overload. Single AM cortisol is inadequate — use 4-point salivary cortisol.
HPA StageCortisol PatternPresentationPriority
Stage 1 — AlarmCortisol ↑↑ all dayWired but tired, insomnia, anxietyAdaptogens (gentle), Mg, phosphatidylserine
Stage 2 — ResistanceHigh AM, low PMMorning OK, 2–4pm crash, light sleepAdaptogens + sleep protocol
Stage 3 — ExhaustionFlat/low all dayProfound fatigue, orthostatic hypotension, salt cravingAdrenal glandulars, DHEA, Vit C 2g, B5
1
Testing4-point salivary cortisol (8am, noon, 4pm, bedtime). DHEA-S. DUTCH test (best — adds metabolites + sex hormones). Optimal AM salivary cortisol: 13–24 nmol/L. Flat CAR = Stage 3/burnout.
2
Stage-Specific AdaptogensStage 1: Ashwagandha KSM-66 300mg BD ★★★ (cortisol ↓ 28%) + Phosphatidylserine 300mg PM + Rhodiola 200mg AM. Stage 2: Ashwagandha + Rhodiola AM + Holy Basil 500mg BD. Stage 3: Licorice root 500mg + Adrenal glandular + Vit C 2g + B5 500mg BD. NEVER stimulants in Stage 3.

cards.title: PCOS — Comprehensive Naturopathic Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Rotterdam Criteria — 2 of 3
(1) Oligo/anovulation (irregular periods) + (2) Clinical/biochemical hyperandrogenism (hirsutism, acne, elevated testosterone) + (3) Polycystic ovaries on ultrasound (≥12 follicles per ovary or ovary >10mL).
InterventionTargetDoseEvidence
Inositol (Myo + D-chiro 40:1)Insulin resistance, ovulation4g myo-inositol + 100mg D-chiro/day★★★★ Strong — restores ovulation in 70%
BerberineInsulin resistance, androgen500mg TDS with meals★★★ Good — equals metformin in some trials
Spearmint teaAnti-androgenic2 cups/day (1 tsp dried)★★★ Good — reduces free testosterone
Vitex agnus-castusLH:FSH ratio, progesterone400–500mg standardised extract OD★★★ Good — regulates cycle
N-Acetyl CysteineInsulin resistance, ovulation600mg TDS★★★ Good — comparable to metformin
ZincAnti-androgenic, insulin30–50mg/day★★★ Good — reduces hirsutism, improves IR
Vit D3Insulin resistance, menstrual regularity4000–6000 IU/day★★★ Good — PCOS patients often severely deficient
Low-GI diet + exerciseInsulin resistance, weight150 min/week + low-GI, high-fibre★★★★ Strong — 5% weight loss restores ovulation in 50%

cards.title: Menopause — Naturopathic Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
SymptomInterventionDoseEvidence
Hot flushes / night sweatsBlack cohosh (Actaea racemosa)40–80mg standardised extract/dayGood — reduces frequency 50%
Hot flushesSoy isoflavones / Red clover40–80mg isoflavones/dayGood — reduces severity
Mood / anxietySt John's Wort + Black cohoshStandard combined doseGood RCT for combination
Vaginal drynessSea buckthorn oil oral + topical Vit E3g/day oral + topicalGood
SleepValerian + Lemon balm + Mg glycinate300mg each at bedtimeGood
Bone protectionCa 1200mg + D3 2000IU + K2 100mcg + Mg 400mgAs stated dailyStrong
CVD protectionMediterranean diet + exercise + omega-3 3gDailyStrong
🟡 Black Cohosh Safety
Black cohosh is NOT oestrogenic — it works via serotonin and dopamine pathways. Safe in oestrogen-receptor-positive breast cancer (unlike phytoestrogens — conflicting data for ER+ cancer). Rare hepatotoxicity (1:1,000,000) — monitor LFTs with long-term use. Use maximum 6 months then reassess. Do not confuse with blue cohosh (Caulophyllum) — different plant, potentially toxic. Avoid in pregnancy and liver disease.
